cards.title: Colon Hydrotherapy — Types & Indications

alerts.label: Definition
alerts.text: Colon hydrotherapy (colonic irrigation) introduces water into the colon via the rectum to cleanse the large intestine. A core naturopathic detoxification modality targeting the "toxic colon" concept.

cards._legacyHtml:
Definition
Colon hydrotherapy (colonic irrigation) introduces water into the colon via the rectum to cleanse the large intestine. A core naturopathic detoxification modality targeting the "toxic colon" concept.
TypeDescriptionVolumePrimary Use
Simple saline enemaWarm saline 0.9% per rectum500ml–1LConstipation, pre-procedure bowel prep
Coffee enemaOrganic coffee solution per rectum500mlLiver detox, pain relief (Gerson therapy)
Herbal enemaTriphala, neem, aloe vera solution500mlBowel dysbiosis, IBS, colitis
Probiotic enemaLactobacillus suspension100–200mlPost-antibiotic dysbiosis, IBD
Colonic irrigationContinuous flow, professional equipment50–60L totalDeep colon cleanse, constipation
Basti (Ayurvedic)Herbal decoction + oil250–500mlVata disorders, joint disease
IndicationTypeFrequency
Chronic constipationSaline / colonicWeekly × 4 then monthly
Detox programmeCoffee / herbalDaily × 7 days (fasting)
IBS / dysbiosisHerbal → ProbioticAlternate days × 2 weeks
Liver diseaseCoffee enemaOD–BD during therapy
Preparation for colon hydrotherapySalineNight before

cards.title: Coffee Enema — Protocol & Evidence

pearls.label: 🟡 Safety Pearl
pearls.text: Coffee enema is CONTRAINDICATED in: bowel obstruction, rectal fissures, severe haemorrhoids, active IBD flare, dehydration, cardiac arrhythmia, caffeine sensitivity, pregnancy. Never exceed 2x/day — electrolyte depletion and dependence risk. Deaths reported from water-only enemas at extreme temperatures. Always ensure body temperature (37°C) and sterile technique.

steps.title: Preparation
steps.text: Boil 1L filtered water with 3 tbsp organic ground coffee for 15 min. Cool to body temperature (37°C). Strain through fine mesh. Never use instant coffee or commercially flavoured coffee.

steps.title: Equipment
steps.text: Sterile enema bag/bucket, lubricated rectal catheter (size 22–24Fr), IV stand or hook. Ensure all equipment sterilised with boiling water or 70% alcohol before and after use.

steps.title: Patient Position
steps.text: Left lateral (Sims) position or knee-chest. Insert catheter 3–4 inches. Hang bag 18 inches above rectum. Introduce 500ml over 5–10 minutes. Patient retains for 12–15 minutes.

steps.title: Mechanism
steps.text: Caffeine and theobromine absorbed via haemorrhoidal veins → portal circulation → liver. Stimulates glutathione S-transferase (700% increase), bile flow, and hepatic detoxification. Palmitic acid opens bile ducts.

steps.title: Post-enema
steps.text: Patient evacuates. Replenish electrolytes — coconut water or ORS. Avoid solid food for 1–2 hours. Monitor for: hypokalaemia (weakness, cramps), rectal irritation, electrolyte imbalance if used daily.

1
Preparation Boil 1L filtered water with 3 tbsp organic ground coffee for 15 min. Cool to body temperature (37°C). Strain through fine mesh. Never use instant coffee or commercially flavoured coffee.
2
Equipment Sterile enema bag/bucket, lubricated rectal catheter (size 22–24Fr), IV stand or hook. Ensure all equipment sterilised with boiling water or 70% alcohol before and after use.
3
Patient Position Left lateral (Sims) position or knee-chest. Insert catheter 3–4 inches. Hang bag 18 inches above rectum. Introduce 500ml over 5–10 minutes. Patient retains for 12–15 minutes.
4
Mechanism Caffeine and theobromine absorbed via haemorrhoidal veins → portal circulation → liver. Stimulates glutathione S-transferase (700% increase), bile flow, and hepatic detoxification. Palmitic acid opens bile ducts.
5
Post-enema Patient evacuates. Replenish electrolytes — coconut water or ORS. Avoid solid food for 1–2 hours. Monitor for: hypokalaemia (weakness, cramps), rectal irritation, electrolyte imbalance if used daily.
🟡 Safety Pearl
Coffee enema is CONTRAINDICATED in: bowel obstruction, rectal fissures, severe haemorrhoids, active IBD flare, dehydration, cardiac arrhythmia, caffeine sensitivity, pregnancy. Never exceed 2x/day — electrolyte depletion and dependence risk. Deaths reported from water-only enemas at extreme temperatures. Always ensure body temperature (37°C) and sterile technique.

cards.title: Colon Hydrotherapy — Contraindications

alerts.label: Absolute Contraindications ★
alerts.text: Bowel obstruction · Bowel perforation risk · Active GI bleeding · Colorectal cancer · Recent GI surgery (<6 months) · Severe haemorrhoids · Rectal/anal fissures · Severe IBD flare · Diverticulitis (acute) · Renal failure · Cardiac failure · Severe dehydration · Pregnancy · Uncontrolled hypertension.

rows: Vasovagal — lay flat, raise legs, monitor BP
Reduce flow rate, allow partial evacuation
Do not force — reduce volume, assess cause

pearls.label: 🟡 Documentation
pearls.text: Always document: patient consent, enema type and volume, temperature verified, duration retained, character of effluent, patient tolerance, any adverse reactions. Maintain sterile technique throughout. Single-use disposable catheters are preferred in professional settings.

cards._legacyHtml:
Absolute Contraindications ★
Bowel obstruction · Bowel perforation risk · Active GI bleeding · Colorectal cancer · Recent GI surgery (<6 months) · Severe haemorrhoids · Rectal/anal fissures · Severe IBD flare · Diverticulitis (acute) · Renal failure · Cardiac failure · Severe dehydration · Pregnancy · Uncontrolled hypertension.
Red Flag During ProcedureAction
Sudden severe abdominal painSTOP immediately — risk of perforation
Bright red rectal bleedingSTOP — refer emergency
Fainting / syncopeVasovagal — lay flat, raise legs, monitor BP
Severe crampingReduce flow rate, allow partial evacuation
Difficulty retaining fluidDo not force — reduce volume, assess cause
🟡 Documentation
Always document: patient consent, enema type and volume, temperature verified, duration retained, character of effluent, patient tolerance, any adverse reactions. Maintain sterile technique throughout. Single-use disposable catheters are preferred in professional settings.

cards.title: Enema Preparation — Step-by-Step

steps.title: Saline Enema
steps.text: Dissolve 1 tsp non-iodised salt in 500ml–1L boiled-cooled water. Temperature 37°C (test on wrist). Equipment: sterile enema bag, lubricated catheter 18–22Fr, IV stand. Patient: left lateral (Sims) position, knees slightly flexed. Hang bag 45cm above patient level. Introduce slowly over 5–10 min.

steps.title: Triphala Herbal Enema
steps.text: Boil 2 tsp Triphala powder in 500ml water for 10 min. Strain well, cool to 37°C. Indicated for chronic constipation, haemorrhoids, colitis, dysbiosis. Triphala is astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial. Retain 10–15 min.

steps.title: Probiotic Retention Enema
steps.text: Mix 2–4 probiotic capsules (50B CFU multi-strain) in 100–150ml warm saline (37°C). Administer in knee-chest position. Retain 20–30 min or overnight. Most effective when colon is empty — give cleansing enema first, wait 30 min, then probiotic. Used post-antibiotic, severe dysbiosis, IBD.

steps.title: Neem Antimicrobial Enema
steps.text: Boil 10–15 neem leaves in 500ml water for 15 min. Strain, cool to 37°C. Neem is strongly antimicrobial and anti-parasitic. Use for: intestinal parasites, Candida overgrowth, chronic dysentery. Retain 10 min. Maximum 2-week course.

steps.title: Post-Procedure Care
steps.text: Hydrate with 300–500ml coconut water or ORS after any enema. Rest 20–30 min. Frequency limits: cleansing enemas max 3x/week; therapeutic herbal OD–BD × 7–14 days; probiotic 3x/week × 4 weeks. Chronic use causes electrolyte imbalance and bowel dependency.
